body:
Binary serializer that converts `LMUVehicleTelemetry` and `LMUVehicleScoring` ctypes structs into compact `.tmu` frames using `struct.pack`.

### Serializer (`LMUPI/lmupi/sharedmem/tmu_serializer.py`)

- **~130 channel catalogue** mapping all relevant telemetry and scoring fields to named channels with type codes (`d`/`f`/`i`/`B`/`?`), units, and extractor lambdas
- **Unit conversions** applied at extraction: Kelvin→°C, rad→deg, m/s→km/h, 0–1→%
- **Configurable channel selection** — record all or a named subset
- **Pre-compiled `struct.Struct`** for zero-overhead frame packing
- **File header** with magic bytes, format version, metadata JSON block, and channel definition table
- **Decode methods** for roundtrip verification

```python
ser = TMUSerializer(channels=["speed", "throttle", "gear", "tire_temp_fl"])
header = ser.encode_header(track="Monza", car="Porsche 963", sample_rate=60.0)
frame = ser.encode_frame(vehicle_telemetry, vehicle_scoring)  # flat binary, ser.frame_size bytes
decoded = ser.decode_frame(frame)  # {"timestamp": ..., "speed": ..., ...}
```

### Tests (`LMUPI/tests/test_tmu_serializer.py`)

20 tests covering helpers, channel selection, encode/decode roundtrips, unit conversions, header parsing, multi-frame sequential decoding, and error cases.

> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Implement binary serializer for shared memory structs</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Description
> Create a serializer that converts LMU shared memory structs into the `.tmu` frame format.
> 
> ## Requirements
> - Map all relevant fields from `LMUVehicleTelemetry` and `LMUVehicleScoring` to channels
> - Use `struct.pack` or similar for efficient binary encoding
> - Support configurable channel selection (record subset of available data)
> - Handle type conversions (Kelvin→Celsius, radians→degrees, etc.)
> - Produce a flat binary frame per sample
> 
> ## Depends On
> - .tmu format spec (#1)</issue_description>
>
